intro: Pseudoclausia gracillima, also known as the Tibetan Globe-flower, is a flowering plant species native to the high-altitude plateaus of the Tibetan Plateau. The plant has been traditionally used in Tibetan medicine to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Pseudoclausia gracillima include flavonoids, terpenoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Tibetan herbal medicine, Pseudoclausia gracillima is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into herbal formulas to treat various health conditions.
